originalTitle: Djibouti's president wins unprecedented sixth term with 97.8% of vote
title: A New Chapter for Djibouti! President Guelleh Wins Again
content:
Hello, Friends! Let's Visit Djibouti!

Hi everyone! It's your kid-friendly news reporter, here to tell you something exciting happening far away in a country called Djibouti! Can you say Djibouti? It's a special place in Africa, near the big blue ocean. Imagine your school. You have a head teacher, right? And maybe even a class monitor! Well, countries also have leaders. They are called presidents or prime ministers. They help everyone in the country, just like your head teacher helps all the students. Today, we're talking about the leader of Djibouti. His name is President Ismail Omar Guelleh. That's a long name! He is 78 years old. That's older than most of your grandmas and grandpas! He has been the president of Djibouti for a very, very long time.

What is an Election?

Sometimes, people in a country get to choose who their leader will be. This is called an election. It's like when you vote for your favorite color in class, or for who gets to be the line leader. Everyone gets to say who they want to be in charge. When people vote, they pick the person they think will do the best job. This is a very important thing! It helps make sure that the country's leader is someone many people trust and like. Did you know? Elections are a way for people to have a voice. Your voice is important!

President Guelleh Wins Again!

Guess what? There was an election in Djibouti recently. And President Ismail Omar Guelleh won! He got a super-duper big number of votes. Almost everyone who voted chose him! Imagine if almost all the kids in your class voted for you to be the line leader. That's how many votes he got! It was 97.8% of the votes. That's very, very close to 100%! It means almost everyone who went to vote, chose him. This means he will continue to be the president of Djibouti. He has been the president for a super long time already, for 27 years! Wow, that's even longer than your parents have been alive, probably! He started being president way back in 1999. Can you imagine? Some of your parents were probably still kids then!

Did You Know?

Djibouti is a small country in East Africa. It's famous for its super salty lakes and its amazing volcanoes. It's a hot place, often with bright sunshine!

Why Did Some People Not Vote?

Now, here's a little bit more about this election. There were other people who wanted to be president too. They are called "opposition candidates." But some of these people decided not to take part in this election. It's like if you had a race at school, but some of the fastest runners decided not to join the race. They might do this if they felt the race wasn't fair, or if they wanted to show that they were unhappy about something. So, when President Guelleh won with so many votes, it was because many people who *did* vote chose him. And also, because the other main people who wanted to be president were not in the race.

What Does a President Do?

A president has a very important job. They help make sure that schools are good, and that everyone has food to eat. They work to keep the country safe and happy. They also meet with leaders from other countries. It's like your head teacher meeting with head teachers from other schools to talk about how to make things better for everyone. President Guelleh will now continue to do these jobs for Djibouti. He will make decisions for the country for another term. A "term" is just a period of time that a leader is in charge. He has been president for six terms now! That's why we say it's an "unprecedented" sixth term – it means it hasn't happened like this before. It's a very special and big number.

More Fun Facts About Djibouti!

Did you know Djibouti is home to a special type of shark called a whale shark? They are the biggest fish in the world, but they are gentle giants! They eat tiny plants and animals in the water. Djibouti is also known for its beautiful coral reefs. These are like underwater cities built by tiny sea animals, full of colorful fish! It's also a place where you can see many unique birds, like flamingos, standing tall on one leg!

Why Is This News Important?

This news is important because the leader of a country plays a big role in everyone's lives. President Guelleh has been leading Djibouti for a very, very long time, and now he will continue. It's good to know about what's happening in different countries, even far away ones like Djibouti. It helps us understand how the world works and how different people choose their leaders. So, that's the news from Djibouti! President Ismail Omar Guelleh will keep leading the country. We hope he continues to do his best for all the wonderful people and places in Djibouti! Thank you for listening! See you next time for more kid-friendly news from Africa!
summary: President Guelleh will continue to lead the country of Djibouti after winning another vote where almost everyone who voted chose him, even though other leaders did not join the election.
whyMatters: This is important because the leader of a country helps everyone, and how leaders are chosen is a big part of how a country works.

originalTitle: Palestinians appalled as Israel approves settlements in occupied West Bank
title: Let's Learn About Homes and Land in a Special Place!
content:
Hello, Amazing Kids of Africa!

Hi everyone! It's your friend reporter, here to share a story from far away. I love telling you about what's happening in the world. Today, we're going to talk about homes and land. They are so important to all of us, right? We will learn about some news from a place far from our beautiful continent of Africa. It's a place where families live, just like you. We will talk about it in a simple way. Are you ready?

Big News About Homes

There's a country called Israel. And next to it is a special piece of land. You can think of it like a big neighborhood or a large farm area. It is called the West Bank. Some grown-ups in Israel have said 'yes' to building many new houses there. They want to build 34 new groups of homes. That's a lot of new houses! It's like building many new places for families to live. But this news has made some other families very worried. These families are called Palestinians. They live in the West Bank too, and they are worried about their homes. Did you know? Our homes keep us safe and warm! They are places where we eat, sleep, and play with our families.

Where is This Place? The West Bank

The West Bank is a piece of land in a big area of the world. This area is called the Middle East. It's not in Africa, but it's good to know about places all over the world. Many, many families have lived in the West Bank for a very, very long time. Imagine your village or your town. That's a special place for you and your family, right? The West Bank is like that for many people. It has schools, markets, and places where people grow food. It's a place full of life and history for the people who live there. Did you know? The Middle East is a region with many different countries and cultures, just like Africa!

Who Are the People Involved?

In this story, we hear about two groups of people. One group is from Israel. The other group is called Palestinians. Both groups of people have families, children, and dreams. They both live in the same big area of the world. Sometimes, like in this story, they don't agree on everything. It's a bit like when you and your friend both want to play with the same toy. You need to talk and find a way to share. Did you know? There are millions of people who live in this area, both Israelis and Palestinians. They all want to live happily.

What Does "Settlements" Mean?

When we hear the word "settlements" in this news, it means new groups of houses or villages. Imagine if someone decided to build many new houses right in the middle of your favorite playing field. Or maybe they built them next to your grandmother's garden! These new houses are planned for the West Bank. They are being built by people from Israel. The problem is that the Palestinian families already live on this land. Or they use this land for their farms. They feel this land belongs to them. So, building new homes there makes them worried. Did you know? Building new houses takes a lot of work and materials, like bricks, wood, and paint!

Why Are People Worried?

The Palestinian families are very worried. They are scared they might lose their own homes. Or they might lose their farms where they grow food for their families. They might lose the land where their children play. Imagine if someone built new houses so close to your home that you couldn't get to your school easily. Or you couldn't reach your favorite tree. That would make you sad and worried, wouldn't it? They feel that building these new houses on their land is not fair. They are worried about their future and where they will live. Everyone needs a safe place to call home. Did you know? Feeling worried or scared is okay. But it's important to talk about our feelings and ask for help.

What Can We Hope For?

This news reminds us that sharing is very important. When we share our toys or our snacks, everyone is happier. Sharing land and homes can be harder, but it's super important for peace. We hope that the grown-ups from both Israel and the Palestinian families can talk to each other. We hope they can listen to each other's worries. We hope they find a way to live together peacefully. Every child deserves a happy and safe home. Let's send our good wishes for peace and understanding to them!

Let's Learn Together!

Even though this news is from far away, it helps us learn important lessons. It teaches us about being kind. It teaches us about sharing. And it teaches us about listening to others. When people listen to each other, they can solve problems. We can all hope for a future where everyone has a safe home and peace in their hearts. Thank you for listening, wonderful kids! Keep being curious and kind! Did you know? Even small children can help make the world a better place by being kind to their friends and sharing!
summary: New homes are planned in a place called the West Bank, and some families there, called Palestinians, are worried about losing their own homes and land.
whyMatters: This is important because everyone needs a safe home and a place to live and play. We hope everyone can share and be happy together.

originalTitle: Israel reprimands Spanish diplomat over detonation of Netanyahu effigy
title: A Big 'Oops!' Between Countries
content:
Hey there, awesome explorers of Africa! It's your friendly news reporter, always ready to share exciting stories from around the world. Today, we have a story about two countries, Israel and Spain, and something that made one of them feel a little sad and upset.

What Happened?

Imagine you have a best friend. Sometimes, your friend might do something that makes you feel a bit unhappy. Well, something like that happened between two countries, Israel and Spain. Israel told Spain that they were not happy about something that happened. It was about a special kind of doll, almost like a big puppet, that looked like Israel's leader. This doll was shown in a way that made Israel feel disrespected. So, Israel told Spain's special helper in their country, a 'diplomat', that they were not pleased. Think of it like telling a teacher that another classmate did something that hurt your feelings. It's a way to say, 'Please don't do that again.'

What is an Effigy?

Okay, so what exactly is this 'special kind of doll' we are talking about? It's called an **effigy**! Can you say 'ef-fih-jee'? It's a bit of a tricky word, isn't it? An effigy is like a model or a doll that looks like a real person. Sometimes people make effigies of famous people, like leaders or sports heroes. They might do it to show respect, or sometimes, to show that they are not happy with something that person has done. In this story, someone made an effigy of the leader of Israel. His name is Prime Minister Netanyahu. Then, this effigy was shown in a way that made Israel feel it was not nice. They felt it was a very disrespectful act. Did you know? In some parts of Africa, people also make figures or dolls for special events or stories. But these are usually for good reasons, like celebrating ancestors or teaching important lessons!

Who is a Diplomat?

Now, let's talk about the special helper from Spain. He is called a **diplomat**. Can you say 'dip-loh-mat'? A diplomat is a very important person. Imagine you are visiting a friend's house in another town. Your parents might send you with a message to give to your friend's parents. A diplomat is a bit like that, but for whole countries! Diplomats are people who live and work in a different country. Their job is to help their home country and the country they are living in be good friends. They talk to each other's leaders and try to solve problems peacefully. They make sure people from their country are safe and happy there. Spain has a top diplomat in Israel, living in a city called Tel Aviv. When Israel was unhappy, they spoke to this diplomat to share their feelings. It's like talking to the grown-up who is in charge of keeping peace between two families.

Why Did Israel Get Upset?

Israel got upset because they felt that blowing up or showing an effigy of their Prime Minister in a disrespectful way was very unkind. It's like if someone made a doll that looked like your teacher or your elder and then did something mean to it. How would that make you feel? When countries have leaders, those leaders represent all the people in that country. So, when someone disrespects a leader, it can feel like they are disrespecting the whole country and all its people. Countries try very hard to be polite and respectful to each other. This helps them stay friends and work together on important things. When something disrespectful happens, it can make building those friendships harder. This is why Israel felt it was important to talk to Spain's diplomat. They wanted Spain to know that such actions are not okay and can hurt feelings between nations. It was a way for Israel to say, 'Please, let's be kind and respectful to each other.' Did you know? Countries often send diplomats to many different places around the world. This helps everyone talk and understand each other better, like having friends in every village!

Why is it Important to Be Kind?

This story reminds us that kindness is super important, not just between you and your friends, but between countries too! When people are kind and respectful, they can work together to make the world a better place. If countries are always fighting or being disrespectful, it's hard to share ideas, help each other when there's a problem, or even just be good neighbours. Just like in your classroom, if everyone is kind, learning is more fun and easier. So, Israel told the Spanish diplomat that they were disappointed. This is called a 'reprimand'. It means they gave a serious telling-off, but in a very polite and formal way. It's a way for countries to say, 'We are still friends, but we need to talk about this problem so it doesn't happen again.' Being kind and respectful is a big lesson for everyone, no matter how big or small you are, or whether you are a person or a whole country! Did you know? The Prime Minister of Israel lives and works in a special building called the Knesset, which is like their parliament. It's where important decisions for the country are made!

What Happens Next?

After Israel spoke to the Spanish diplomat, it is hoped that Spain will understand Israel's feelings. Hopefully, everyone will remember to be respectful and kind, even when they have different ideas. This kind of talk helps countries understand each other better. It helps them fix small problems before they become big ones. It's all about talking things through, just like you would with a friend if you had a misunderstanding. And that's our story for today, friends! Remember to always be kind and respectful to everyone you meet, no matter where they are from. It helps us all live happily together on our amazing planet Earth!
summary: Israel told Spain's special helper that they were unhappy about an effigy of Israel's leader being shown disrespectfully, reminding everyone that kindness and respect are important between countries.
whyMatters: This story matters because it shows us that even big countries need to be kind and respectful to each other, just like friends, so everyone can get along and live peacefully.
